item.title: A crank formula for π 摘要
item.description:
John D. Cook examines a crank formula for π that claims to relate π to physical constants like Planck's constant, the speed of light, electron mass, and the total energy of the universe. He simplifies the expression using Planck's blackbody law, finding it reduces to a form involving h, c, and E/m. However, the term 'total energy of the universe' is ambiguous and undefined, and the resulting value for E (about 2.85×10^19 Joules) is not physically justified. The formula is not dimensionless, suggesting it only works with nonstandard units. Cook concludes the formula is likely nonsense.
标签: π, crank formula, physics, physical constants, Planck's constant, blackbody radiation, dimensional analysis, pseudoscience, mathematics, John D. Cook

item.title: Powering up a module from the IBM 604: an electronic calculator from 1948 摘要
item.description:
The IBM 604 Electronic Calculating Punch, introduced in 1948, was a programmable calculator that bridged electromechanical accounting machines and electronic computers. It used about 1250 vacuum tubes, including thyratron tubes for high-current switching, and featured innovative pluggable modules that simplified manufacturing and maintenance. Though not a full computer (lacking loops, memory, and stored programs), it was affordable at $550/month and performed 60 operations per second, including multiplication and division. Over 5600 units were produced, and IBM's experience with the 604 led to successful vacuum-tube computers like the IBM 650.
标签: IBM 604, history of computing, vacuum tubes, thyratron, pluggable modules, electromechanical, electronic calculator, IBM, 1940s computing, computer architecture

item.title: Mux — Video for Developers 摘要
item.description:
Mux, a video infrastructure provider trusted by Patreon, Substack, and Synthesia, is sponsoring Daring Fireball. Mux offers AI workflows called Mux Robots that automatically unlock data from video files for tasks like summarization, caption translation, and moderation. Developers can configure these workflows once, and they run on new uploads. A promotional code FIREBALL provides an extra $50 credit for new signups.
标签: Mux, video, AI, workflows, sponsorship, developer tools, infrastructure, summarization, captioning, moderation

item.title: The Laplace limit 摘要
item.description:
The article discusses solving Kepler's equation using power series (Lagrange, 1771) versus sine series (Bessel). The power series solution converges only for eccentricity e < 0.6627 (the Laplace limit), while the sine series works for all e < 1. This means Lagrange's method fails for highly eccentric orbits like Sedna's (e=0.8549). The Laplace limit arises from the radius of convergence of the power series, not from astronomy. The article explains how to compute the limit numerically and presents the series expansion using Lagrange inversion.
标签: Kepler equation, Laplace limit, power series, convergence, eccentricity, Lagrange inversion, Bessel functions, celestial mechanics, numerical methods, Python

item.title: Aitken acceleration before Aitken 摘要
item.description:
Kepler solved his equation M = E - e sin(E) via fixed-point iteration E = M + e sin(E), which converges quickly for small eccentricities (e.g., Jupiter's e=0.05 needs 2 iterations) but slowly for high eccentricities (Halley's comet e=0.967 needs 16 iterations). In 1891, C. F. W. Peters discovered a faster algorithm using three steps per iteration, achieving 10^-10 residual in 3 iterations for Halley's comet. Peters' method was later recognized as a special case of Aitken's delta-squared acceleration (1926).
标签: Kepler equation, fixed-point iteration, numerical methods, series acceleration, Aitken acceleration, Peters algorithm, orbital mechanics, history of mathematics, convergence, eccentricity
